entry.title: Re: Can Tax Code Be Changed Without Reversal in S/4HANA P...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Unfortunately, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, once a supplier invoice is posted, you can't change the tax code even if the tax amount is correct. This is because tax codes affect legal and financial reporting, so SAP locks them in to ensure compliance and a clean audit trail.

So what can you do ?

Option 1: Reverse and repost the invoice (this is the proper way)

This is SAP’s standard process:

  • You reverse the original invoice (using the “Manage Supplier Invoices” app, for example).
  • Then, you create a new invoice with the correct tax code.

Yes, it’s a bit of extra work, but it’s the only way to ensure your tax reporting stays accurate and compliant.

 

Option 2: Post a manual adjustment (only in some cases)

If the tax amount is correct and you’re only concerned about the tax code for internal purposes (like analytics), you could:

  • Leave the original invoice as-is.
  • Post a manual journal entry to reclassify the tax amounts between accounts.

Just note: this won’t change the tax code on the original invoice, and it won’t show up correctly in your VAT reports. So this is more of a workaround for internal corrections—not for anything that affects tax filings.

 

What you can’t do :

  • There’s no way to edit the tax code on a posted invoice.
  • No app, no API, no back-end workaround for that in the public cloud version.
  • Customizing isn’t possible either it’s a locked field after posting.

 

In short:

If the tax code is wrong, even if the amount is right, you’ll need to reverse and repost the invoice to fix it properly.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Automatic PO not created in S/4 HANA Public Cloud aft...
entry.content:
Hello, 

if I were you, I would check the following: 

1. Workflow or Configuration Issues

  • Flexible Workflow Not Configured: Automatic PO creation often depends on the correct setup of flexible workflows for RFQs and supplier quotations in S/4HANA Public Cloud. If the workflow is not configured or not triggered, the PO will not be created automatically.

  • Release Strategy: The RFQ or quotation may require approval before a PO can be generated. Ensure that the workflow allows for automatic release or that all necessary approvals are completed.

2. Integration and Master Data Settings

  • Missing or Incorrect Integration Settings: Ensure that the integration scenario between Ariba Sourcing and S/4HANA Public Cloud is fully configured, including communication arrangements, system connections, and relevant user authorizations.

  • Master Data Prerequisites: The material master and vendor master records must be set to allow automatic PO creation. This includes:

    • "Automatic PO" flag enabled in both material and vendor master records.

    • Source list and purchase info records maintained for the material and vendor combination.

3. Process and Data Consistency

  • Quotation Data Issues: If the awarded quotation contains inconsistencies (e.g., missing or mismatched GL accounts, currencies, or required fields), the PO creation may fail silently or trigger errors.

  • Budget or Commitment Issues: In some cases, budget or commitment management settings may prevent PO creation if commitments are not properly reversed or managed.

Hope it will help you.

Best regards,

Patryk Walędziak

entry.title: Re: 批量运行结算WBS
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Yes, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, as of now, the replacement app "Run Settlement: Actual" indeed only supports single project execution.

For batch settlement, currently there is no standard Fiori app that supports mass execution of project settlement in the same centralized way as the deprecated app did.

However, you have two possible alternatives:

  1. Use Background Job Scheduling (App: "Schedule Project Settlement - Actuals")
    • You can schedule settlement as a recurring job for multiple projects using selection criteria (e.g. project type, status).
    • This is found under "Schedule Project Settlement - Actuals" or within the "Job Schedule for Projects" app.
    • Go to App "Schedule General Ledger Jobs" > Choose Job Template "Project - Settlement: Actual".
  2. Create a Custom CDS View + RAP App (Advanced)
    • If mass execution is essential in your process, your technical team can build a custom RAP-based app or automation that loops through multiple WBS elements using available APIs.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Start via SAP Start Booster – No Apps or P...
entry.content:
Hello @Nithesh_Yadav 

Thanks for the detailed context and screenshots. You're not the first to run into this; it’s a common blocker when setting up SAP Mobile Start via the SAP Start booster under the Foundation Plan.

Solution

1st option,

Upgrade to Standard Plan (Recommended)

  • Gives you access to Content Manager.
  • Let’s you import Fiori content and assign it to users.
  • Apps will then show up correctly in both SAP Start and Mobile Start.

2nd option,

Manual Workaround (Not ideal)

  • You can try manually creating tiles and linking S/4HANA apps via URL.
  • But it’s time-consuming, doesn’t scale well, and Mobile Start may not recognise them

Summary

Issue

Explanation

No apps showing

Foundation Plan doesn’t support content federation or assignments

Booster is successful, but empty UI

Booster sets up trust & connection, not app visibility

SAP Mobile Start empty

Because apps aren’t federated or assigned via Work Zone

Solution

Upgrade to SAP Build Work Zone – Standard Plan

 

Visit these SAP useful sites Below

Learning Journey – SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition

SAP Blog – Real-World Setup

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Technical Error when calling Project Element Badi
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

If you're getting "Technical Error when Calling Project Element BAdI" when changing the Project Manager, it likely means there's a problem in a custom BAdI implementation triggered during that change.

Here's how to troubleshoot:

1. Go to the “Custom Logic” app and check any BAdIs related to project elements or project header. Look for custom code that might be failing.

2. Use the “Application Log” app to find more details about the error (search by project or time).

3. Check if the new Project Manager is valid (e.g., is set up as a Business Partner with the right roles).

4. If needed, temporarily disable the custom logic and try again—if the error goes away, the issue is in the BAdI.

5. Review the code for missing checks or unhandled exceptions.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Address error Germany: “Max length 6 of target field exceeded. Source field address_information stat
entry.content:
As we all know we have different countries supported by EC and we can select different countries in EC address. Depending on the country selected the address fields are populated.

While doing replication of address for EC to ECP it is necessary to have the length to be defined as per SAP standards in EC also it is necessary to have fields mapped as per standard. This does not mean we cannot add custom fields it can be done but than as always it is better to keep it simple by using standard fields.

We have used all standard fields in EC for country Germany and the same have been mapped to ECP.However while replicating employee from EC to ECP we were receiving the error ““Max length 6 of target field exceeded. Source field address_information state DEURhineland-Palatinate”

First look at this error and we did check in “Manage business Configuration” and there was no field called “state” defined for Germany.

Then we did get puzzled of how we are getting an error with a field being referred which is not enabled.

Next step we did was pull in a report from reporting .We came to know the beauty of the reports when we downloaded the report . The report showed that during imports the value in field state was being updated which resulted in above error. On removing the data from “state” and reimporting in EC and running replication the error was solved

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Start via SAP Start Booster – No Apps or P...
entry.content:
Hi @Chuma,

Thank you so much for the detailed and structured response , this really helps clarify things!

We suspected that the Foundation Plan's limitations around content federation and role assignments were likely the root cause, and your confirmation aligns perfectly with our findings.

We had hoped the SAP Start booster would take care of all required configurations, including app visibility, but as you rightly pointed out, it seems the booster handles trust and connectivity, not content provisioning or launchpad content assignments - which require Content Manager, only available in the Standard Plan.

We did consider the manual workaround (creating tiles with target mappings via URLs) but as you mentioned, it’s not sustainable for a scalable setup—especially in a production-grade mobile enablement scenario via SAP Mobile Start.

We will now evaluate the upgrade to SAP Build Work Zone – Standard Plan, as it appears to be the cleanest and most maintainable solution moving forward.

Really appreciate you taking the time to share your insights and confirm the technical boundaries here.

Thanks again!
Nithesh T S

entry.title: SAP Fiori Innovation Day Bangalore -- Connection, Gratitude, AI, and Local Engineering Pride
entry.content:
The first-ever SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Bangalore was a tremendous success, bringing together 200+ participants for what became the largest SAP Fiori Innovation Day yet. This event provided local developers an opportunity to connect with customers, showcase our latest AI-powered innovations, and help us understand where to focus our development efforts.

In this post, I'll discuss the key sessions, share insights from our presentations, and highlight the remarkable contributions from our local development community.

Engineering Pride from Our Local Contributions to the UX Foundation Portfolio

We started with Ioannis Grammatikakis, SVP for UX Foundation at SAP, providing our UX strategy overview for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. His vision about intelligent UX and the humanness aspect that creates customer loyalty resonated strongly with our audience.

Ioannis Grammatikakis, SVP for UX Foundation at SAP, presenting the UX strategy and vision for SAP S/4HANA

We then demonstrated the latest AI functionality embedded across the SAP S/4HANA portfolio:

  • My Home: AI-powered app recommendations, intelligent insights cards, and Joule integration for personalized experiences
  • List Report: Natural language filtering that translates user requests into precise filter criteria
  • Object Page: AI summarization features allowing users to select sections and share results with colleagues

It was gratifying to see our local Bangalore team members demonstrating these features they directly contributed to developing. The audience engagement was particularly strong, with participants asking about integrating S/4HANA systems with Microsoft Teams services and bot platforms.

Hands-on Experience with Hands-on Experience with SAP Fiori Tools

Our hands-on sessions led by Gaurav Rawat showed how SAP Fiori tools in Business Application Studio (BAS) addresses 80% of common use cases. Participants experienced the complete development cycle from template selection through deployment, with tools automatically handling metadata, annotations, and dependencies. The interactive nature of the sessions sparked excellent questions from participants, including how to configure simple filters in SAPUI5 Freestyle applications, setting up SAP Fiori tools extensions in BAS, and whether SAP Fiori apps developed through SAP Fiori tools can be containerized using Docker.

Participants working with SAP Fiori tools and exploring AI-powered development features

Interactive Demo Booths: Hands-on Exploration

Beyond the main stage presentations, participants had the opportunity to explore multiple demo booths throughout the day, each focusing on different aspects of SAP Fiori development and user experience:

Leverage SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library: Participants explored how to identify apps strategic for their business needs by leveraging the comprehensive library of available SAP Fiori applications and learning about installation options.

Intelligent Entry Experience to SAP S/4HANA: Demonstrations showcased the journey from first steps to daily excellence, featuring the latest My Home page in SAP S/4HANA systems, which serves as the standard entry page providing a personalized and unified user experience.

Seamless and Collaborative Experience with Technology-Agnostic Cards: This session focused on creating seamless user interactions through technology-agnostic cards that enhance collaboration and overall application usability.

AI-Assisted Easy Filter: A deep dive into redefining the filtering experience through innovation, featuring natural language filtering capabilities and AI-powered search functionality.

SAP Fiori Elements Fast-Track App Development: Best practices and strategies for boosting developer efficiency and building applications that can grow with business needs using SAP Fiori elements. Participants were particularly interested in the availability of public documentation for these app generation capabilities.

Interactive Demo Booths

Mindset's AI-Powered Innovation: Reimagining SAP Fiori User Experience

Our partner Mindset Consulting presented their session "Reimagining SAP Fiori: AI-Powered User Experience" showcasing their innovative document summary generator using LLM models. Their solution demonstrates how AI can automatically process and summarize documents, with the generated summaries being stored in HANA DB for efficient retrieval and reuse. This demonstration highlighted how partner solutions can extend SAP Fiori applications with advanced AI capabilities, showing practical applications of intelligent document processing in enterprise scenarios.

Developing Applications is a Team Sport, So Connections Matter

Modern enterprise applications require collaboration between business owners, user researchers, designers, enterprise architects, and developers—with AI tools increasingly becoming team members. The diverse skills of our Bangalore participants perfectly matched today's fusion development teams. The energy and engagement throughout the event will directly influence our future innovations.

SAP Fiori Innovation Day team

Gratitude for Making SAP Fiori Innovation Day a Huge Success

This event's success came from the energy and engagement of 200+ participants. Their questions, comments, and feedback provide invaluable guidance for future innovations.

Thanks to all SAP presenters for their valuable contributions. Special thanks to Mindset Consulting for sponsoring and their presentation on Reimagining SAP Fiori User Experience, and the SAP Inside Track team for integrating our UX track into their larger community event.

Watch for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Your Region

  • Dallas, Texas, USA -- October 17, 2025 as part of the ASUG chapter meeting
  • Paris, France -- December 10, 2025

Continue Your Learning

  • Subscribe to the bi-monthly SAP Fiori Development Newsletter
  • Sign up for the SAP S/4HANA UX Roundtable in India (starting in summer 2025)
  • Join the monthly SAP Fiori Development Roundtable for U.S. and Europe if that fits your schedule better

entry.title: Re: Quantity field is not updating in ACDOCA table during...
entry.content:
Hi @msshihab1987 ,

Please refer to KBA note 27922 - Info: Settlement types and quantity settlement - SAP for Me

If account-based profitability analysis is used, the logic for updating consumption quantities is the same as described above...

...the system updates primary cost element quantities and activity quantities if these are settled according to the cost element or if the settlement cost element has a unit of measure. If only the quantities for the revenue elements are required in the profitability analysis, then only the revenue elements should be settled according to the cost element because this generates a large amount of data.

I hope it helps.

BR,

Filipe

entry.title: Add header text to uploading budget template PSM
entry.content:
I’m reaching out for assistance with an issue we’re facing in our SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment (Public Sector scope).

We’re currently uploading budgets for specific cost centers using the “Public Sector: Run Application Job” functionality. The job runs successfully and the budget documents appear in the “Manage Budget Documents” app. However, when we go to the Manage Budget Documents app, we see that the document description in the header is always set to “Generated Automatically” (see attached screenshot). We would like to add our own description during the upload (For example: Budget round June 2025), so that the documents are easier to recognize afterwards. 

 

So far, I’ve tried the following:

 

  1. Adding a custom column in the upload template → either ignored or causes an error
  1. Renaming the job title → only shows in the job log, not as the document header text
  1. Creating budget documents manually via the app → works, but not feasible for bulk uploads
  1. Looking into APIs or extensibility options → seems overly complex or unavailable in standard public cloud

 

My questions:

 

  1. Is there a standard way to include a document header text when uploading budgets via the application job?
  1. If so, how can this be configured or enabled?
  1. If not, what would be the best alternative to ensure uploaded documents are easily distinguishable?

 

Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: SAP Best Practices for Solution Quotation Management (7HB)
entry.content:
With SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, we have delivered an SAP Best Practices scenario for Solution Quotation Management.

7HB - Solution Quotation Management with SAP CPQ 

The SAP S/4HANA Quotation Management with SAP CPQ offers customers the capability to create quotations for sales and service contract products in SAP CPQ. After the customer accepts the proposal the SAP CPQ Quote creates Sales Order and Service Contract automatically. The linkage document in between is the SAP Solution Quote document, which holds information about SAP CPQ Quote ID and the document ID of Sales Order and Service Contract.

(fig 1. SAP CPQ Integration with Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA )

Let’s start by looking in some more detail at the SAP Configure, Price, Quote integration with Solution quotation Management in SAP S/4HANA , Private Edition. SAP CPQ enables salespeople to create complex product configurations with tailored pricing and customized attributes with ease. The seamless pre-defined integration of SAP CPQ and solution quotation  in SAP S/4HANA  is implemented via BTP Cloud Integration and encompasses the transfer of pricing and product configuration data via CPS, and master data replication via MDI. The new scope item titled Solution Quotation Management with SAP CPQ (7HB) has been created for which contains the standard mapping logic required for implementing the integration.

(fig 2 SAP CPQ Integration with Solution Order Management process flow diagram)


The integration enables salespeople to leverage the full set of CPQ capabilities to create customized quotes. In the SAP CPQ front end, the order is configured and calculated based on the selection of available attributes. Once the quote is confirmed by the customer and the order is placed, it is handed over to solution order management in SAP S/4HANA  for further processing.  When the Solution Order has been processed, the status in CPQ is also updated to show that the order has been placed for fulfilment. The integration initially supports configurable and non-configurable sales and service contract products, with further product types to be added in future releases. Via the standard OData API, the initial creation, as well as read, update and delete operations are supported.

How Customers and Partners Can Give Input to the Roadmap of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

We would like to provide you with a comprehensive overview of how and where you can raise your voice to influence developments in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition going forward.

For more information, see the blog post from Anja Wilde here.

For general roadmap information please check the SAP S/4HANA Roadmap.

 Thanks for reading this blog post.

If you are also interested in other Lines of Business and Industries for this new SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 release, I would like to draw your attention to my link collection blog – The Link Collection.

If you want to learn more about SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, join our Partner Community. This group focuses on providing enhanced learnings and periodic updates concerning the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition product. Our goal is to facilitate collaboration with your peers and SAP subject matter experts.

 

For more information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, check out the following links:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release info: sap.com/s4hana
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Community here
  • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel here
  • Inside SAP S/4HANA Podcast here
  • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition here
  • Help Portal Product Page here
  • Feature Scope Description here
  • What’s New here

entry.title: Re: MDGF- How to edit a GL account
entry.content:
Hello @former_member836676 , 

Don't go Mass change option to edit a GL account. 
To edit GL in MDG, First search for the existing GL account in NWBC-> Financial Accounting->Search G/L Account Centrally. 
It will display the list of the GL accounts. 
Now, open the GL account you want to edit and you can see edit option beside each category(Account Details, Account Descriptions, etc.,) . 

Click on edit, and it will ask for the CR type and edition


Now select your required CR type, Edition and you can edit the GL account data and submit the CR for approval. 
Thanks, 
Rajeshwar

entry.title: Re: Taking online exam with seperate device for webcam
entry.content:
Hi @AaronANZ 

It has to be on the same machine as outlined in Getting certified - FAQs "What are the technical requirements for taking an exam?", however it can also be an external webcam that is connected to the laptop via USB.
It cannot be a mobile phone as mobile phones are not allowed to be used during the exam. so the advice is to get an external webcam if the built-in webcam does not work and connect it to the laptop via USB.
Kind regards
Margit
